title: Helianthemum neopiliferum
intro: Helianthemum neopiliferum, also known as a species of rockrose, is a flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of the Himalayas. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as potential antimicrobial and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Helianthemum neopiliferum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. Helianthemum neopiliferum is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and support immune function.
taxonomy: Helianthemum neopiliferum is a species of flowering plant in the family Cistaceae. It belongs to the genus Helianthemum, which comprises about 120 species of rockrose plants. The species Helianthemum neopiliferum is native to the mountainous regions of China. This plant is classified as a perennial shrub, known for its pink to white flowers and grayish-green foliage. Helianthemum neopiliferum is also known as a local endemic species found mainly in the Sichuan Province of China.
